title: The Mask Prediction Branch
concept: The mask branch in Mask R-CNN is a small Fully Convolutional Network that predicts a binary mask for each class on each region.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What mathematical technique does RoIAlign use to avoid coordinate rounding?
exercises.explain: Correct! Bilinear interpolation allows precise coordinate sampling.

cards.title: Zooming in on the crop
cards.body: Once RoIAlign extracts a clean, aligned feature map (like 14x14) for a proposed object box, we need to create the mask. We don't want a dense layer here because we need to preserve the spatial grid. So, how do we process this cropped feature map? We run a mini FCN inside the branch!

cards.title: The Mask Branch
cards.body: The Mask Branch is a small Fully Convolutional Network applied to each RoI. It uses convolutional layers to process features and outputs a $K imes m imes m$ tensor, where $m imes m$ is the resolution (e.g. 28x28) and $K$ is the number of classes.

cards.title: Painting within the stencil
cards.body: The mask branch is like a painter who is handed a small, stencil-aligned board (the RoI). The painter only has to paint one object inside that stencil, outputting a black-and-white mask indicating what is object vs. background.

cards.title: Class-specific masks
cards.body: If there are 80 classes, the mask branch outputs 80 different 28x28 binary masks for a single box. The classification branch decides which class the object is (e.g., 'Car'), and we select the 'Car' mask as the final output.

cards.title: No class competition
cards.body: Unlike semantic networks that use softmax (where classes compete for each pixel), the mask branch uses a sigmoid function on each pixel. This decouples mask generation from class prediction, making it much easier for the model to train.

cards.title: Mask Branch Resolution
cards.body: The output of the mask branch:

right.text:
Size: 28x28xClass Count
Binary masks (0 or 1 per pixel)

exercises.prompt: What is the structure of the mask branch in Mask R-CNN?

options.text: A single fully connected dense layer
A series of max pooling operations

exercises.explain: Correct! The mask branch is a mini FCN to preserve spatial layout.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The mask branch uses softmax to make classes compete for each pixel.
exercises.explain: False. It uses sigmoid per pixel, decoupling class prediction from mask generation.

exercises.sentence: The mask branch outputs a binary mask of size m x m for each of the K ___.
exercises.explain: It outputs one binary mask per class, allowing the classification branch to choose the final mask.

exercises.prompt: What function is applied to each pixel in the mask branch output to get a binary mask?
exercises.explain: Sigmoid is applied to each pixel to predict object vs. background independently.

exercises.prompt: Why does Mask R-CNN output a separate binary mask for each of the K classes, rather than a single combined multi-class mask?

options.text: It decouples mask prediction from class prediction, allowing the model to generate high-quality masks without class competition.
It reduces the number of parameters in the convolutional layers.
It allows the model to run without backpropagation.
It makes the model compatible with low-resolution images.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Decoupling class prediction (done by the classification branch) from mask prediction makes learning simpler and much more stable. Next: how do we calculate the loss for all these branches?
